pattern: The number of days needed falls monotonically as capacity grows, so binary search the smallest capacity whose greedy day count fits the deadline.
hints: If a given capacity ships everything in time, any larger capacity also does — so the feasible capacities form a clean threshold you can search.
For a fixed capacity, greedily fill each day until the next package would overflow, then start a new day; this gives the exact days that capacity needs.
Binary search capacity between the heaviest single package and the total weight, picking the lowest value whose greedy day count is at most days.

edge_inputs.description: one package, one day, capacity equals that weight
as many days as packages so each ships alone
one day forces capacity equal to the total
heaviest package dominates the lower bound

stress.description: weight list with a tight deadline so the optimal capacity sits just above the heaviest package
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    weights = [rng.randint(1, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    days = rng.randint(max(1, n // 4), n)
    return [weights, days]
stress.note: many days keeps the oracle's linear search short
